replies.msg:
各位大英帝國做緊嘢／將會做嘢嘅巴絲打，歡迎入嚟呢到鳩噏扮下工

Q&A credit to 滑蛋豬仔飯

Q: 如果無BC又想係英國打工係咪好難?

A:
通常會比working visa一係大公司
D細公司仔都見過有批不過當然會少D

老老實實渣BNO/特區係就係難D不過都唔係無可能
都識得有人搞得掂個visa
以前有同學會係cv度寫明話要working visa
如果間公司都肯in你既話多數即係肯批
唔洗浪費大家時間
大小公司都有 試下無妨

再唔係學其他巴打講
申請左working holiday做住先都未嘗不可
當然之後整唔整到tier 2 working visa就唔敢講
不過起碼都有2年比你係度做下野儲下經驗先

如果做開比較專業行業既朋友其實拎左working holiday visa先，再轉tier 2既機會都幾樂觀，都識幾個朋友都係咁，好似都係做design / I.T.之類 (by 賽麗娜Serena)


Q: 想問下英國見工係咪同香港一樣都係上網報?

A:
如果你話搵工過程其實同香港差唔多
睇你咩工啦
普通黎講大公司既話都係cv cover letter -> online test -> phone in-> assessment centre ( 可以好多round) -> final in
細公司就少幾個步驟咁


Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)

Q: 下年打算去working holiday 會唔會好難搵工同搵地方住（想住反係zoom1,2) 聽人講就話最好係香港搵定先去係好定唔好 我就做開酒吧個邊會唔會香港人 真心求教

A:
英國bar tender既需求量其實好大 因為佢pub既數量基本上同香港7仔有得揮 甚至係正常既餐廳入面都會有間pub仔/bar比人淨飲野吹水
所以你有經驗根本唔洗驚 去到英國任何一個地方都一定搵到野做

講我自己經驗啦 我就做好typical既西餐廳既 入面全部鬼佬
因為要升supervisor 所以gm要求我識哂餐廳所有部份 包括floor hatch同bar
Bar嗰邊我完全係一張白紙 連用咖啡機都唔識 基本上淨係識開水喉倒果汁呢d白痴都識做既野
去到依家講緊一個在既時間 叫做忙起上黎可以打哂三邊 聽日冇人返比人編左去自己睇bar

我覺得做bar最緊要係識同客吹水 尤其係餐廳bar
好似我地咁 係住宅區 長期都有熟客日日黎打"等" 真係日日風雨不改黎報到
黎到冇野做咪同bar tender吹水囉 所以你一定要好吹得

其餘都冇乜野擔心 驚咪去indeed search下囉 一大炸請緊人 (by 小野屋杏)

Q: 英國黎緊暑假畢業，準備搵工 （宜家4月中係咪太遲)

A: 唔遲
雖然有啲係截左
但仲有好多
留學生有優勢 就係唔怕relocation
成個英國既工你都apply得
宜家真係開始send cv都唔會太遲既
好多大公司如果唔係grad scheme既話其實一路都請緊人
以前好多同學都係8/9月先有offer
有D直頭下年1月先有工開
所以唔洗擔心既肯踏出第一步搵工就ok (by 馬壯FN2)

係英國搵緊60k都係覺得香港好


I wish I am earning £60k a year!

You only need 6 years in order to get British Citizenship - 5 years under Tier 2 for Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) and a year after having ILR to apply for British Citizenship.

The 6 years investment you put in will -

1) Get you a second citizenship just in case things go belly up in Hong Kong.
2) You can travel to the US visa free.
3) Your children will be British Citizens when they are born.

It is up to you whether to continue your stay in the UK.

If you are a British Citizen by descent, please remember to retain evidence of your stay in the UK like council tax bills, P45 and P60. You can register your overseas born child to become a British Citizen after three year continuous residence in the UK prior to the birth using form MN1

Really? I thought if my descendants want to obtain British passport, he/she must be born in UK.


Giving birth in the UK with one parent being a British Citizen by descent is the safest way in getting British Citizenship for the next generation. If you are planning to do this, I would suggest you to give birth to your child in Northern Ireland. By doing this, you can also get Irish citizenship for the next generation.

Under section 3(2) of the British Nationality Act 1981, an overseas born child of a British Citizen by decent can register as a British Citizen if -

a) the British Citizen by descent parent lived in the UK for a continuous period of three years prior to the birth of the child.

b) The three years residence in the UK - the British Citizen by descent parent cannot be absent from the UK for more than 270 days in total.

c) the father or mother of the British Citizen by descent parent (i.e. the child's grandparent) must be a British Citizen otherwise than by descent at the time of the birth of the child's British Citizen by descent parent.

Read page 10 - 11 of the link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/583385/MN1_Guide_January_2017.pdf

So a little example -

If the grandfather received British Citizenship otherwise than by descent in 1992 under the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990.

He had a child in Hong Kong the year after (1993).

The child became a British Citizen by descent at birth.

The child went to live in the UK between 2005 and 2015.

The child then went back to Hong Kong and gave birth to the grandchild in 2016.

The grandchild appears to have a strong case for registration under section 3(2).

However, from the wording of the 1981 Act, those who received their British Citizenship under section 1(4) of the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990 (as minor children of the main applicant) may not be able to register their overseas born children as British Citizens under section 3(2) as their parents were not British Citizens otherwise than by descent at the time of their birth.

As I am not a lawyer or a register immigration adviser in the UK, you will need to seek professional help before making any decisions.

I would play safe - you don't want to be question or even refused entry to the UK by immigration officer at the airport. Stay in the UK until you get your T2 visa.

Also you should consider using your BN(O) passport for your Tier 2 visa as you can register to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA) which is a cheaper method than naturalisation (£1163 for registration using form B(OTA) vs £1282 for naturalisation using form AN - both 2017 prices)

@HKG3 巴

Any difference of the government fee/ charge when applying Tier 2 visa between hksar / bn(o)? Some sharing added that no fee is charged if you are using BN(O) for Tier-2


BN(O) passport holders still need to pay the same visa fee!

I may sound like a broken down record by now, but if you are planning to get a Tier 2 visa with £40k p.a. job and planning to get British Citizenship as a goal, then I would suggest you to use your BN(O) passport to get your Tier 2 visa. Here are the advantages BN(O) passport holders have over HKSAR passport holders -

1) BN(O) passport holders with long term UK visa (greater than 6 months) are not required to register with the local police force where they live while HKSAR passport holders, as Chinese nationals, are required to register with the local police force where they stayed and to re-register if move to a new police force area. Registration with the police cost £34.

2) BN(O) passport holders are entitle to register to vote in all UK elections. Being on the electoral roll helps you to build up your own credit history in the UK.

3) Once you get Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR), you, the BN(O) passport holder, can apply for British civil service posts open to Commonwealth citizens while HKSAR passport holders can only apply for British civil service jobs once he/she becomes a British Citizen.

4) As I said in my earlier post, BN(O) passport holders are entitle to register to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA) which is a cheaper method than naturalisation.

Please note that ILR can be revoked if you lived outside of the UK for more than 2 years. It is recommended that you get British Citizenship before returning to Hong Kong for good.

想問下係咪一定要做3年野先計 讀書唔得


Studying is fine - you are the one that need to provide the evidence to the Home Office. However, please note that if you want to register your child as a British Citizen under section 3(2) of the 1981 British Nationality Act, you, the British Citizen parent of the child, require to be a British Citizen by descent at the time of your own birth. This means that if you have a Home Office registration certificate under the 1990 British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act, you may not register your overseas born child under section 3(2). Please see below for details -

(2)A person born outside the United Kingdom and the qualifying territories shall be entitled, on an application for his registration as a British citizen made while he is a minor, to be registered as such a citizen if the requirements specified in subsection (3) or, in the case of a person born stateless, the requirements specified in paragraphs (a) and (b) of that subsection, are fulfilled in the case of either that person’s father or his mother (“the parent in question”).

(3)The requirements referred to in subsection (2) are—

(a)that the parent in question was a British citizen by descent at the time of the birth; and

(b)that the father or mother of the parent in question—

(i)was a British citizen otherwise than by descent at the time of the birth of the parent in question; or

(ii)became a British citizen otherwise than by descent at commencement, or would have become such a citizen otherwise than by descent at commencement but for his or her death; and

(c)that, as regards some period of three years ending with a date not later than the date of the birth—

(i)the parent in question was in the United Kingdom or a qualifying territory at the beginning of that period; and

(ii)the number of days on which the parent in question was absent from the United Kingdom and the qualifying territories in that period does not exceed 270.

好似喺


To get Indefinite Leave to Remain in the UK using a Tier 2 (General) visa, you
will need to meet the income requirement is over £35k p.a. unless your job was in the shortage occupation list for the last 6 years.

https://www.gov.uk/settle-in-the-uk/y/you-have-a-work-visa/tier-2-general-visa

Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) in the UK is not 英籍! The Home Office can revoke your ILR if you were -

1) sentenced to prison in the UK for 12 months or more

2) lived outside of the UK for more than 2 years

You will need to have ILR for at lease a year before you can either register (using B(OTA) form for BN(O) passport holders only) or naturalise (using form AN for HKSAR passport holders) to become a British Citizen. Registration using form B(OTA) is a cheaper method than naturalisation. Form B(OTA) registration to become a British Citizen costs £1163 (2017 prices) while naturalisation using form AN costs £1282 (2017 prices).

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/607005/NationalityFeesLeaflet_2017.pdf

我想搭單問一問, 如果我係英國讀左4年大學 但我又冇揾到工

要我留多一年係唔難 我可以再讀MASTER 但學生tier 4 visa計唔計落去個5年到?
To be eligible to register under the five-year residence requirement you must:

have been resident in the United Kingdom for at least five years; this is known as the residential qualifying period; and
have been present in the United Kingdom five years before the date of your application; and
have not spent more than 450 days outside the United Kingdom during the five-year period; and
have not spent more than 90 days outside the United Kingdom in the last 12 months of the five-year period; and
have not been in breach of the immigration rules at any stage during the five-year period; and
be free from immigration time restrictions for the last 12 months of the five-year period of your stay in the United Kingdom.The residential qualifying period will be worked out from the day we receive you application. You must show you were present in the United Kingdom at the start of this period. For example, if we received your application on 25 November 2005, you would have to show that you were in the United Kingdom on 26 November 2000.

我仲有特衰
Followings are the requirements you need to meet to be eligible for registration as a British citizen by descent.

You must:

be a British National (Overseas);
not hold any other citizenship or nationality;
not have given up any citizenship or nationality;
not have lost any citizenship or nationality by action or inaction.
It is possible to be a citizen or national of a country even if you have never held a passport issued by the authorities of that country. You should make sure this does not apply to you before you make your application.

It is not that confusing.

You will not get British Citizenship based on 5 years' residence in the UK as a student.

This is because you will not meet the requirements for registration as you are still subject to UK immigration control.

be free from immigration time restrictions for the last 12 months of the five-year period of your stay in the United Kingdom

You will need to have Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) before you can apply for British Citizenship. You can get ILR using the long residence rules, you will need to have 10 continuous legal residence in the UK in order to get ILR via the long residence rules. Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/long-residence

What kind of 銀行文職 you are looking for? What passport do you have?

If you are not a British or EEA citizen, you will need a visa to work in the UK. You can get a Working Holiday visa if you have BN(O), HKSAR or Canadian passport and under the age of 31.


多謝巴打回覆，拎特衰passport 係咪都要申請左visa先？
想aim 銀行business analyst 果種full time job


You will need a visa to work in the UK. The easiest one to get is a Working Holiday visa.

If you hold a HKSAR passport and is under the age of 31, you can apply for an UK working holiday visa. However, you will need a Certificate of Sponsorship (CoS) as a HKSAR passport holder. You can get a CoS from the Hong Kong Labour Department, but as you just missed the second round application for CoS, the next round would be in 2018.

http://www.whs.gov.hk/en/partners.php?location=UK

On the other hand, if you have a BN(O) passport, you will not need a CoS from the Hong Kong Labour Department and can apply for an UK working holiday visa at any time.

If I am having any children, I will try and make sure that the birth will happen in Northern Ireland. In Northern Ireland, you still get NHS health care and the child will get Irish citizenship (with at lease a parent being a British Citizen). Irish citizenship is a useful thing to have as -

1) Irish citizens will continue to get freedom of movement in the EU after Brexit.

2) It is a lot easier to pass Irish citizenship down the generations.

You only need to give birth to the child in Northern Ireland in order to get Irish citizenship (as long as one of the parent is a British Citizen). I am not telling people to move to Northern Ireland for good - but only temporarily for childbirth.
